title: Potential SQL Injection
description: User input is directly interpolated into a SQL query string, allowing for SQL injection attacks.
code:
const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '${req.body.username}'`;
await db.execute(query);
fix:
const query = 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?';
await db.execute(query, [req.body.username]);
recommendation: Always use parameterized queries or an ORM to prevent SQL injection vulnerabilities.

title: Hardcoded API Key
description: A sensitive API key is hardcoded directly into the source code.
fix:
const STRIPE_SECRET_KEY = process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY;
const stripe = require('stripe')(STRIPE_SECRET_KEY);
recommendation: Move all sensitive credentials to environment variables and use a .env file for local development.

title: Uncaught Promise Rejection in Async Loop
description: Using .forEach with an async function does not await the promises, potentially causing race conditions and uncaught rejections.
recommendation: Use a for...of loop for sequential execution, or Promise.all(items.map(async (item) => ...)) for parallel execution.

title: Frequent DOM Query in Loop
description: Querying the DOM inside a loop causes unnecessary reflows and performance degradation.
recommendation: Cache DOM queries outside of loops and use document fragments or batch updates to minimize reflows.

title: Vulnerable Package Version (lodash)
description: The current version of lodash (4.17.15) has a known Prototype Pollution vulnerability.
recommendation: Update lodash to version 4.17.21 or higher to patch the Prototype Pollution vulnerability.
